summary.intent: Integrate Mantine UI component library into the Electron/Vite app and progressively enhance the UI with better styling, interactivity, and a GitHub-inspired dark/light theme.
summary.outcome: Mantine was fully installed and configured with PostCSS; all screens and components were migrated from custom primitives to Mantine components; the sessions table gained copy-to-clipboard cells, activity indicators, and expandable checkpoint dropdowns; and the overall app adopted a GitHub-inspired AppShell layout with persistent dark/light mode toggle.

repo: Project structure: Electron app lives at gossamer/app/src/renderer/src/, server at gossamer/src/server.ts, DB schema at gossamer/prisma/schema.prisma.
Mantine requires postcss.config.cjs (not .js) with postcss-preset-mantine for CSS variable support in the Vite/Electron build.
Activity color thresholds are shared logic: <2 min = green (active), <15 min = orange (idle), else gray (inactive) — used in both SessionTree and SessionRow.
Checkpoints endpoint is /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints and was already available for lazy fetching.
CheckpointMetadata has no createdAt, but CheckpointSessionMetadata has createdAt DateTime? — server.ts needed updating to expose it.
localStorageColorSchemeManager from Mantine persists color scheme preference across Electron sessions.

code.finding: CopyCell component pattern: wraps Table.Td, uses absolute-positioned ActionIcon on hover with paddingRight:28 to avoid text overlap; shows checkmark briefly on copy. Row expansion fetches checkpoints lazily on first open and caches in state.
GitHub-inspired dark palette mapped to Mantine dark[7..0]: base #0d1117 → #e6edf3. defaultColorScheme set to 'dark' with localStorageColorSchemeManager.
Had to add createdAt from CheckpointSessionMetadata to the checkpoints list endpoint response — it was not previously included.

workflow: Build verification (electron-vite build) after each major change is an effective sanity check before moving to the next step.
Reading all existing screens/components before migrating avoids redundant reads and allows planning the full migration in one pass.
Writing full file rewrites (Write tool) rather than incremental edits is preferred for large-scale component migrations.

friction: User interrupted a feature mid-implementation (adding a single latest checkpoint ID column) and pivoted to a different design (expandable dropdown with last 5 checkpoints), requiring the in-progress work to be discarded.
PostCSS config required .cjs extension specifically — not standard .js — due to the Vite/Electron module format constraints.
createdAt was missing from the server checkpoint response and required a server-side fix before the UI could display it, discovered only when implementing the Checkpoints card list.
open_items: Light mode styling was added via toggle but no screenshot-driven design review was done for light mode specifically — may need visual polish pass.

The 'make it look more like this' screenshot reference was interpreted by the assistant without the actual image being visible in the transcript — fidelity to the original design intent is unverified.
SessionDetail, CheckpointDetail, CheckpointTimeline, and SessionTree screens were not restyled in the final AppShell/GitHub-theme pass — only Checkpoints and Layout were updated.
No tests were added or updated to cover the new CopyCell behavior, lazy checkpoint fetching, or color scheme persistence.
